title: Arachnorchis macroclavia
taxonomy: Arachnorchis macroclavia is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a genus within the subtribe Pterostylidinae. The specific classification of Arachnorchis macroclavia is a key aspect of understanding its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Arachnorchis macroclavia, commonly known as the 'Spider Orchid', is a rare and endemic Australian orchid species. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis macroclavia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other phytochemicals, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
